description:
You are a cloud-platform architect who has just finished migrating several production services to a new AWS
    region.  
    A small infrastructure repository that lives at  

        /home/user/projects/infra-scripts  

    already exists and contains:  

        • a plain-text file called “VERSION” whose **only** line is the current
          semantic version of the repo (presently “2.4.7”).  
        • a Terraform file at
          /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/terraform/main.tf that still
          hard-codes the tag “v2.4.7” everywhere it references internal reusable
          modules.  
        • a CHANGELOG.md that follows the “Keep a Changelog” format and already
          has historical sections up to **## [2.4.7] – 2023-06-02**.  
        • an (empty) directory /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/logs that is
          writable by the current user.

    Task  
    ----  
    1. Perform a **minor** semantic-version bump from 2.4.7 → 2.5.0.  
    2. Meticulously update **all** references of the old tag inside
       terraform/main.tf (there are exactly two of them) so that they now read
       “v2.5.0”.  
    3. Overwrite the content of /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/VERSION so it
       contains a single line with nothing but:

            2.5.0

       followed by a single trailing newline (UNIX line ending).  
    4. Pre-pend a new section to CHANGELOG.md that follows the
       “Keep a Changelog” specification *exactly* and looks **verbatim** as
       shown below (date must match the day the agent runs the commands – use
       the system’s current UTC date).  Replace YYYY-MM-DD with that date:

            ## [2.5.0] - YYYY-MM-DD
            ### Added
            - Migrated `edge-proxy` and `billing-api` services to the `eu-central-1` region.
            - Introduced environment variable `REGION_AWARE=true`.

            ### Changed
            - Updated Terraform module version references to `v2.5.0`.

            ### Fixed
            - Corrected IAM role policy typo that prevented ECS tasks from assuming roles.

       The new entry **must** be placed directly above the old
       “## [2.4.7] – 2023-06-02” section, with a single blank line separating
       the two headings (as shown).  
    5. Create a plain-text audit log file
       /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/logs/version-bump.log.  Append **one**
       new log line whose exact format is:

            YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | old:2.4.7 | new:2.5.0 | files: VERSION, terraform/main.tf, CHANGELOG.md

       where the timestamp is the current UTC time obtained from
       `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`.  Do **not** write any additional lines or
       characters to this file.

    Verification details the automated test will check  
    ---------------------------------------------------  
    • /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/VERSION contains exactly “2.5.0\n”.  
    • Every occurrence of “v2.4.7” inside
      /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/terraform/main.tf has been replaced by
      “v2.5.0”, and no other file inside /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/
      contains “v2.4.7”.  
    • CHANGELOG.md contains the new section **exactly** as shown above,
      positioned at the top of the document, and the date matches the current
      system date in UTC.  
    • /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/logs/version-bump.log exists, is
      non-empty, has precisely **one** well-formatted line, and that line
      references the correct old and new versions and the correct file list.

    You do **not** need elevated privileges; all paths are writable by the
    current user.  Work entirely from a Bash shell inside the container.  Use
    any workflow you like (echo, sed, printf, etc.), but be sure the final
    repository state meets the exact requirements above.
truth:
Initial repository state before the agent starts
    -----------------------------------------------
    Directory tree:

    /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/
    ├── VERSION
    ├── terraform/
    │   └── main.tf
    ├── CHANGELOG.md
    └── logs/

    1. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/VERSION  
       Content (single line, LF terminated):
       2.4.7\n

    2. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/terraform/main.tf  
       Minimal content (only the relevant fragments shown – there are exactly
       two occurrences of v2.4.7):

           module "network" {
             source = "git::https://example.com/network.git?ref=v2.4.7"
           }

           module "monitoring" {
             source = "git::ssh://git@example.com/monitoring.git//modules/agent?ref=v2.4.7"
           }

    3. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/CHANGELOG.md  
       Content up to start of file:

           # Changelog
           All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

           ## [2.4.7] - 2023-06-02
           ### Added
           - Initial release of production blue/green deployment pipeline.

    4. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/logs/  
       Directory exists and is writable; it is empty when the scenario starts.

    Expected repository state after successful completion
    -----------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/VERSION  
       Content: “2.5.0\n”

    2. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/terraform/main.tf  
       All “v2.4.7” replaced by “v2.5.0”; no remaining “v2.4.7”.

    3. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/CHANGELOG.md  
       The agent must prepend the exact text block described in <task> (with the
       correct current date).  All spacing, headings, and bullet points must
       match.

    4. /home/user/projects/infra-scripts/logs/version-bump.log  
       Single line with ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, followed by literal text:
           " | old:2.4.7 | new:2.5.0 | files: VERSION, terraform/main.tf, CHANGELOG.md"
       Example acceptable line (timestamp will vary):
           2023-11-03T14:26:55Z | old:2.4.7 | new:2.5.0 | files: VERSION, terraform/main.tf, CHANGELOG.md

       No other lines allowed.

    The test harness will:
      • read the VERSION file and assert equality to "2.5.0\n"
      • grep for "v2.4.7" recursively under /home/user/projects/infra-scripts and expect zero hits
      • verify CHANGELOG.md contains “## [2.5.0] - <today’s date>” as its first
        changelog section and that the block content matches byte-for-byte after
        normalizing <today’s date>
      • check that version-bump.log exists, has exactly one line that matches
        the regex:
          ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z \| old:2\.4\.7 \| new:2\.5\.0 \| files: VERSION, terraform/main\.tf, CHANGELOG\.md$
